content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 22, 1988

Melvin G. Cooper, Executive Director
Alabama State Ethics Commission
817 South Court Street
Suite 2B Annex
Montgomery, Alabama 36104

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-105

Dear Mr. Cooper:

You have asked whether, under our law, spouses of state legislators may be employed by special interest groups paid lobbyists. Our law, the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 1/, does not prohibit the spouse of a state legislator from being a paid lobbyist. However, the legislator would have certain financial disclosure and disqualification obligations under the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions (See Duffy Advice Letter, No. A-85-126, copy enclosed.)

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: John G. McLean
Counsel, Legal Division

_____________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

DMG:JGM:jaj

Enclosure

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

STATE OF ALABAMA
STATE ETHICS COMMISSION
817 SOUTH COURT STREET
SUITE 2B ANNEX
MONTGOMERY, ALABAMA 36104

March 7, 1988

TELEPHONE (205) 261-2997

Melvin G. Cooper
Executive Director

Howard E. McKenzie
Assistant Director

James J Hicks, M.D.
Cordell Wynn, Ph.D
Sandra K. Martin, Ph.D.
James H Anderson, Esq
J Ray Warren


Mr. John Keplinger, Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Keplinger:

     Once again, we need your help, and we need it fast.  Write
us quickly and answer this question:

     "Under your Ethics law, may spouses of State Legislators
be employed by special interests groups as paid lobbyists?"

     If you have Advisory Opinions or rulings relative to this
question, we would greatly appreciate receiving copies of those
documents.

     You have been mighty helpful to us in the past, and we thank
you in advance for your assistance this time.  A stamped, self-
addressed envelope is enclosed for your convenience.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Melvin G. Cooper
                                        Executive Director

MGC/bh

Encl

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 16, 1988


Melvin G. Cooper
Executive Director
Alabama State Ethics
Commission
817 South Court Street, Suite 28 Annex
Montgomery, AL 36104

Re: 88-105

Dear Mr. Cooper:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on March 11, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel
